desc: Cuenca pairs colonial architecture with high-altitude Andean climate, making it a strong base for culture-focused trips and nearby Cajas National Park hiking.
hls: UNESCO old town and riverside promenades
Reliable café-and-coworking scene for long stays
Quick access to Cajas páramo trails
Lower humidity than tropical Ecuador hubs

todo.name: Historic center walking circuit
todo.desc: Cover Plaza Calderón, cathedral interiors, and flower market streets.

todo.name: Pumapungo Museum + ruins
todo.desc: Combine the museum with adjacent Inca site and ethnobotanical gardens.

todo.name: Cajas National Park day hike
todo.desc: Take an acclimatized day trip for lagoon trails and highland scenery.

todo.name: Panama hat workshop
todo.desc: Visit traditional toquilla weaving studios around Cuenca.

todo.name: Río Tomebamba sunset stroll
todo.desc: Walk both banks and stairways through El Barranco.

todo.name: Mercado 10 de Agosto lunch
todo.desc: Sample mote, hornado, and fresh juices in the central market.

wifi.note: Good fiber availability in central neighborhoods and serviced apartments.

prac.fltNote: UK/Ireland travelers typically connect via Quito, Guayaquil, Madrid, or Bogotá; Cuenca's domestic legs are frequent but can tighten in Easter and July-August periods.

airports.name: José Joaquín de Olmedo International Airport (Guayaquil)

prac.currency: Ecuador local currency (check live rates before booking).
prac.lang: Local language plus tourism-level English in main visitor areas.
prac.tz: Check local timezone and DST rules for exact travel dates.
prac.visa: Confirm current entry/transit rules for your passport before booking.
alerts: At 2,500+ m elevation, first-day exertion can cause altitude fatigue—hydrate and keep plans light.
Afternoon showers are common even in drier windows; carry a shell for park and old-town walks.
Intercity roads to Guayaquil can face fog or landslides in wetter months, so avoid very tight same-day flight connections.

sweet: Jun-Sep for sunnier mornings and easier Andean hiking, with Apr-May and Oct-Nov workable for city breaks.
